releaseBoundary: This registry distinguishes evidence already displayed in the public CB-CAP release from official sources prepared for future governed ingestion. A listed source is not evidence that its measures are currently displayed.

includes: GNIS populated communities when no Census place result is available
towns represented as Census county subdivisions
GNIS populated communities when no Census place or subdivision result is available

zipLinkedAreas.label: Census ZCTA
zipLinkedAreas.boundary: A ZIP Code Tabulation Area is a Census statistical area, not a USPS delivery route. Not every ZIP Code has a ZCTA, and a ZCTA should not be presented as a postal service area.

sources.label: TIGERweb current geography services
sources.release: Current TIGERweb service; the committed county boundary snapshot uses the January 1, 2025 vintage

freshness.cadence: On-demand lookup cached for up to seven days; committed state and county snapshot refreshed through the governed release workflow

coverage.scope: 50 states, the District of Columbia, and 3,144 current county equivalents in the committed release; supported subcounty geographies are queried from TIGERweb

sources.purpose: Authoritative names, identifiers, geography types, and boundaries for nationwide search and map joins.
limitations: A Census place or county subdivision may differ from a postal city name or local administrative usage.
Geography verification does not establish that compatible health, workforce, broadband, or local program evidence is available.

sources.label: Geographic Names Information System populated places

freshness.cadence: Fallback name lookup cached for up to seven days when Census place and county-subdivision search returns no match

coverage.scope: Federally recognized domestic populated-place names in the 50 states and District of Columbia

sources.purpose: Find named communities, including populated places that are not represented by a current Census place or county-subdivision result.
limitations: A GNIS populated place is a named point, not a Census statistical area, municipal boundary, postal city, provider market, or service area.
The current release verifies the name and source only; it does not infer a community health profile or transfer county estimates to the community.

sources.label: PLACES County Data, GIS Friendly Format, 2025 release
sources.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.release: Released December 4, 2025; source years vary by measure

coverage.scope: 3,143 of 3,144 current county equivalents have a matched CDC PLACES row in the committed release
coverage.unit: county equivalents with a matched source row

sources.purpose: Model-based area estimates for chronic conditions, prevention, health-related social needs, and accessibility context.
limitations: PLACES values are model-based population estimates, not diagnoses, person-level facts, observed service demand, or program results.
Measures use different eligible populations and source years and must be interpreted one measure at a time.
Missing values remain missing and are never converted to zero.

sources.label: PLACES Place Data, GIS Friendly Format, 2025 release
sources.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.release: Released December 4, 2025; source years vary by measure

freshness.cadence: Exact place GEOID lookup cached for up to one day

coverage.scope: Compatible incorporated-place and Census-designated-place estimates are checked after a verified Census place is selected

sources.purpose: On-demand place-level population-health and health-related social-needs context.
limitations: A Census place may cross county boundaries and may differ from a postal city name.
A verified place with no compatible PLACES row remains visible as geography only.

sources.label: PLACES ZCTA Data, GIS Friendly Format, 2025 release
sources.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.release: Released December 4, 2025; source years vary by measure

freshness.cadence: Exact ZCTA lookup cached for up to one day

coverage.scope: Compatible Census ZCTA estimates are checked after a verified ZCTA is selected

sources.purpose: ZIP-linked population-health and health-related social-needs context where a Census ZCTA exists.
limitations: A ZCTA is not a USPS ZIP Code delivery route and should not be presented as an exact ZIP Code boundary.
A verified ZCTA with no compatible PLACES row remains visible as geography only.

sources.label: American Community Survey 2024 five-year estimates

freshness.cadence: Annual release; not yet ingested into the public CB-CAP evidence view

coverage.scope: Official national ACS five-year program; selected variables and margins of error require governed ingestion and validation

sources.purpose: Future local context for demographics, language, disability, broadband subscription, vehicles, income, poverty, housing, and other community conditions.
limitations: No ACS variable is displayed from this registry entry in the current public release.
Production use must preserve margins of error, universe definitions, release years, and comparability warnings.

sources.label: Area Health Resources Files
sources.release: HRSA dashboard data as of January 29, 2026; underlying variable years differ

freshness.cadence: Annual; not yet ingested into the public CB-CAP evidence view

coverage.scope: Official county, state, and national health-workforce and health-resource files

sources.purpose: Future county workforce, facility, training-pipeline, resource-scarcity, and delivery-system context.
limitations: No AHRF value is displayed in the current public release.
Underlying years, profession definitions, and county availability differ by variable and must travel with every displayed measure.

sources.label: Health Workforce Shortage Areas

freshness.cadence: Official download is refreshed daily; not yet ingested into the public CB-CAP evidence view; production ingestion must record extract date, designation status, and applicable update date

coverage.scope: Official geographic, population, and facility HPSA and MUA/P designation data

sources.purpose: Future primary-care, dental-health, mental-health shortage and medically underserved designation context.
limitations: No shortage designation is displayed or inferred in the current public release.
A designation applies only according to its official type, discipline, geography or population, status, and effective dates; it is not a CB-CAP score.

sources.label: National Broadband Map and Broadband Data Collection
sources.release: Current FCC Broadband Data Collection public map and downloads

freshness.cadence: Providers file availability data twice yearly; not yet ingested into the public CB-CAP evidence view

coverage.scope: National fixed and mobile broadband availability reported through the FCC Broadband Data Collection

sources.purpose: Future digital-readiness context for telehealth preparation, low-data pathways, hub placement, and public-system planning.
limitations: No FCC broadband value is displayed in the current public release.
Availability is reported by providers and differs from adoption, affordability, device access, digital skills, service quality, or an individual's experience.

sources.label: 2023 Rural-Urban Continuum Codes
sources.agency: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service

freshness.cadence: Periodic classification release; not yet ingested into the public CB-CAP evidence view

coverage.scope: All 3,144 counties and county equivalents in the 50 states and District of Columbia in the 2023 classification
coverage.unit: county equivalents in the source classification

sources.purpose: Future rural and metropolitan context for peer comparisons and place-sensitive planning.
limitations: No Rural-Urban Continuum Code is displayed in the current public release.
County classifications do not describe every community within a county and should not replace tract-level or local context.

sources.label: Community planning for health assessment and health improvement planning
sources.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

freshness.cadence: Review when the official guidance changes

coverage.scope: Official planning guidance; not a statistical dataset

sources.purpose: Frames how CB-CAP can organize secondary evidence, planning questions, accountable ownership, and measurement for CHA and CHIP discussions.
limitations: CB-CAP does not create, approve, or replace an official CHA or CHIP.
Official planning requires community participation, local primary and administrative data, asset and resource inventories, accountable governance, and human priority-setting.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Coverage and affordability
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Ask where coverage, benefits understanding, eligibility, or cost may interrupt a provider-led pathway.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The displayed measure is an area-level estimate for adults ages 18-64 without current health insurance; it is not an eligibility determination or a count of people seeking help.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Reliable transportation
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Test whether local schedules, distance, mobility support, remote preparation, or hub placement warrant local investigation.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The estimate does not identify a transportation route, provider location, resident, or remedy.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Food stability
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Frame questions about trusted non-clinical supports and chronic-disease education context.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The estimate is not a household determination, service referral, or measure of program availability.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Housing stability
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Ask which community and public-system partners should validate and respond to housing-related pathway interruptions.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The estimate does not identify housing status, eligibility, or a specific service gap.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Utility stability
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Consider how household utility instability could affect digital access, device charging, and pathway continuity.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The estimate does not identify a household, utility account, or eligibility for support.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Social connection
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Frame community-engagement and trusted-starting-point questions without inferring an individual's circumstances.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: This measure is not available in the integrated 2025 place profile contract and is never inferred for a place.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Disability and accessibility context
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Plan accessible interfaces, accommodations, communication options, home-based configurations, and inclusive engagement.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: Disability and disabled people are not barriers. This measure is excluded from the pathway-barrier percentile and from the composite planning calculation.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Digital connectivity and readiness
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Future evidence can support low-data design, telehealth preparation, hub placement, device support, and digital-readiness planning.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The current release displays no broadband availability, subscription, affordability, device, or skills measure.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Workforce and service capacity
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Future evidence can support provider-led pathway readiness, workforce development, partner recruitment, and shortage-context review.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The current release displays no provider count, service capacity, HPSA, MUA/P, or workforce forecast.

barrierTaxonomy.label: Rural and geographic context
barrierTaxonomy.planningUse: Future peer groups can distinguish rural and metropolitan county context without treating rurality as a deficit.
barrierTaxonomy.boundary: The current release displays no rural classification, and a county classification cannot describe every community within that county.

chaChipSupport.boundary: CB-CAP is a secondary-evidence and workflow support layer. It does not decide priorities, establish causality, allocate funding, inventory every local asset, or replace community participation, health-department governance, an official CHA, or an official CHIP.

stages.label: Assess
stages.cbcapSupport: Surface source-cited area estimates, comparison context, coverage, missingness, and source limitations.
stages.humanDecision: Choose the assessment scope and add local primary data, administrative records, community experience, assets, and resources.

stages.label: Validate
stages.cbcapSupport: Organize questions about whether local evidence confirms, complicates, or contradicts a displayed signal.
stages.humanDecision: Engage affected communities, public-health leaders, providers, and partners to interpret the evidence.

stages.label: Prioritize
stages.cbcapSupport: Preserve comparable measures and transparent assumptions without ranking unlike indicators as a decision.
stages.humanDecision: Set priorities through the community's approved governance, equity, feasibility, and accountability process.

stages.label: Act
stages.cbcapSupport: Structure actions, owners, pathway partners, safeguards, resources, and review dates.
stages.humanDecision: Approve and carry out actions within each organization's authority, licensure, policy, and community commitments.

stages.label: Measure and learn
stages.cbcapSupport: Keep source versions, assumptions, measures, missingness, and review points visible over time.
stages.humanDecision: Evaluate implementation, explain changes responsibly, and revise the plan through accountable public governance.

workforceReadiness.boundary: These dimensions describe the governed integration plan. They are not current findings, recommendations, provider directories, capacity estimates, or promises of service availability.

dimensions.label: Licensed workforce context
dimensions.planningQuestion: Which workforce measures and profession definitions are current enough to support local pathway-capacity discussions?
dimensions.boundary: Counts do not prove availability, acceptance of new patients, licensure for a particular service, or participation in SozoRock Health.

dimensions.label: Shortage designation context
dimensions.planningQuestion: Which current primary-care, dental-health, mental-health, HPSA, or MUA/P designations apply to the selected geography or population?
dimensions.boundary: Only the official HRSA designation, type, discipline, status, population, geography, and effective date may be displayed.

dimensions.label: Facilities and training pipeline
dimensions.planningQuestion: What facility and training-pipeline context should be validated before a county designs a workforce response?
dimensions.boundary: A public file does not establish operational capacity, partnership interest, program quality, or a verified local asset inventory.

dimensions.label: Digital and telehealth readiness
dimensions.planningQuestion: Where should local partners validate broadband, affordability, device, language, literacy, privacy, and workflow readiness?
dimensions.boundary: Broadband availability or subscription alone does not measure a person's readiness, skills, privacy, trust, or ability to use a provider's platform.

systemsIntelligence.framing: The operating model adapts themes from Oluwabiyi Adeyemo's Rethinking Rural Governance: move from fragmented reporting toward source discipline, connected workflows, operational foresight, community transparency, accountable learning, and anticipatory planning.
systemsIntelligence.boundary: This is a conceptual product architecture, not a source for public health estimates and not evidence that a county, partner, workflow, workforce feed, or institutional learning program is active.

traceability.rule: Every interpretation or planning lever must retain the source identifier, method, status, limitations, and human-review rule. A missing source or method blocks publication.

maturityModel.boundary: The maturity path is a design and governance framework, not a score assigned to any county or institution. The current public release demonstrates structured integration foundations; it does not claim full systems or institutional intelligence.

operatingComponents.label: Governed data architecture
operatingComponents.boundary: Current means the public source contracts, lineage, release hashes, coverage, and missing-data rules are implemented; it does not mean every planned source is integrated.

operatingComponents.label: Performance analytics
operatingComponents.boundary: The current release provides population comparisons and planning scenarios, not program-performance findings or causal evaluation.

operatingComponents.label: Governed feedback loop
operatingComponents.boundary: No county, resident, or partner feedback record is collected by the public preview.

operatingComponents.label: Workforce capability
operatingComponents.boundary: No workforce count, availability finding, training assessment, or readiness score is displayed.

operatingComponents.label: Public transparency
operatingComponents.boundary: Sources, methods, limitations, coverage, missingness, and export context are visible; publication does not replace community interpretation or public accountability.

capabilities.label: Governed data integration
capabilities.currentRelease: Versioned Census geography, CDC PLACES evidence, source lineage, missingness, confidence intervals, and release hashes.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Add ACS, HRSA, FCC, USDA, local CHA/CHIP, and verified operating-model data only through versioned contracts and source review.
capabilities.boundary: A registered future source is not an integrated measure.

capabilities.label: Workforce analytics
capabilities.currentRelease: The source and governance contract is documented; no workforce finding or value is displayed.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Ingest selected HRSA AHRF measures and shortage designations with variable-level years, definitions, status, and coverage.
capabilities.boundary: Counts and designations do not prove appointment availability, provider participation, local capacity, or program performance.

capabilities.label: Operational foresight
capabilities.currentRelease: Users can inspect transparent population-equivalent planning scenarios based on displayed estimates and user-selected assumptions.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Add locally approved capacity inputs, review points, and scenario comparisons without presenting them as predicted demand.
capabilities.boundary: Scenarios are not forecasts of diagnoses, future cases, service demand, clinical capacity, or outcomes.

capabilities.label: Community transparency
capabilities.currentRelease: Sources, vintages, methods, coverage, missingness, limitations, comparison context, and printable briefs remain visible.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Add accessible evidence drawers, local-source review status, decision records, and community feedback routes.
capabilities.boundary: Publication of a measure does not substitute for interpretation with affected communities.

capabilities.label: Maturity diagnostics
capabilities.currentRelease: No county or institution is scored for maturity.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Co-design a non-punitive, evidence-based readiness rubric for data, governance, workforce, cybersecurity, accessibility, workflow, and human review.
capabilities.boundary: No current dashboard value is an institutional maturity grade.

capabilities.label: Institutional learning
capabilities.currentRelease: Saved views and planning drafts remain local to the browser; no institutional learning record is collected.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Add approved action ownership, review dates, measure versions, decision history, and learning cycles behind role-based access.
capabilities.boundary: The public preview is not an audit trail, case-management system, resident record, or performance-management system.

capabilities.label: Anticipatory planning
capabilities.currentRelease: Source-backed comparisons and bounded scenarios can help a planning group identify questions before selecting a response.
capabilities.nextGovernedStep: Connect approved local evidence, workforce, broadband, assets, operating models, and review thresholds with human-governed decision workflows.
capabilities.boundary: CB-CAP does not autonomously set priorities, allocate funds, recommend clinical action, or replace CHA/CHIP governance.

allowed: Apply transparent user-selected assumptions to a displayed source estimate.
Show a population-equivalent planning range when the source supplies a compatible confidence interval.
Support discussion of non-clinical readiness, hub placement, digital support, language access, workforce preparation, and provider-led pathway capacity.
Export the source, vintage, method, assumptions, geography, and limitations with the result.
prohibited: Predict individual health outcomes, diagnoses, future cases, service demand, or clinical capacity.
Present a scenario as an observed count, causal estimate, government designation, funding formula, or recommended allocation.
Generate an uncertainty range when the source provides no compatible interval.
Turn missing or suppressed data into zero or infer a value from neighboring geographies.
scenarioPolicy.humanReview: A planning group must review source definitions, assumptions, local evidence, community priorities, governance, licensure, privacy, feasibility, and accountable ownership before acting.
